titleStmt.title: SII 38.560: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 560. (A.R. No. 560 of 1912.) TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT. On the west wall, Narasiṁhasvāmin shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.hi: King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartti Vīrarājēndrachōḻa Year 3: Siṁha, śu. di. 10, Monday, Aviṭṭam = 30th July 1246 A.D. but the tithi was 15 not 10.

summary.p: This inscription records that Ayiravaṇṇan Āḷkoṇḍavilli alias Tirukkachchi Nambidāsaṉ, a merchant of the nagaram Pūvirundavalli, gifted one twilight lamp to the god Siṅgapperumāḷ (of the temple) at Tirunīrmalai. A priest of the temple Nayimisai Yagñanārāyaṇa Bhaṭṭaṉ Āyappiḷḷai had received one Gaṇḍagōpalan-māḍai and agreed to burn the lamp.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvirarājendracoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 3vatu simhanāyaṟṟu pū
rvapakṣattu daśamiyum tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa Aviṭṭattu nāḷ tirunirmalaināyanār ciṅkapperumāḷukku
pūviruntamallinakarattu vāṇiyaril Ayiravaṇṇan Āḷkoṇṭa villiyāṉa tirukkaccinampidāsaṉ vaitta can
ti viḷakku 1 Iccantiviḷakku Oṉṟukku Ikkoyil nampimāril nayimicai yajñanārāyaṇa bhaṭṭaṉ Āyappiḷḷaiyeṉ kai
koṇṭa kaṇṭakopālaṉ māṭai 1 Immāṭai Oṉṟuṅkoṇṭu Ittiruviḷakku cantirātittavarai celuttakkaṭaveṉ .
Digital edition of SII 38.560 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
